Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
fix: change sameSite cookie attribute from ''strict'' to ''lax'' for HTTP compatibility

## Issue
Login not working when accessed via HTTP or through reverse proxies like Traefik.
Users experienced immediate redirect back to login page after authentication.

## Root Cause
sameSite=''strict'' cookies have compatibility issues with:
- HTTP (non-HTTPS) connections
- IP address access (e.g., http://82.25.97.15:3000)
- Reverse proxy setups without proper X-Forwarded-Proto headers
- Cross-site navigation patterns

## Fix
Changed cookie sameSite attribute from ''strict'' to ''lax'' in login route.

sameSite=''lax'' provides:
- Excellent CSRF protection (blocks cross-site POST/PUT/DELETE)
- Works with HTTP and HTTPS
- Compatible with reverse proxies
- Industry-standard recommendation
- Cookie sent on top-level navigation

## Security Impact
No security reduction - ''lax'' is the recommended standard and provides
robust CSRF protection. The application still has:
- Server-side session validation
- Rate limiting (5 attempts per 15 min)
- HttpOnly cookies
- Secure flag (with HTTPS)
- Session expiration
- Input validation

## Files Changed
- app/api/auth/login/route.ts - Cookie sameSite: ''lax''
- HOTFIX_LOGIN_COOKIE.md - Detailed hotfix documentation
- TRAEFIK_SETUP.md - Reverse proxy integration guide

## Testing
Verified login works with:
- HTTP access via IP address
- Direct port access (no reverse proxy)
- Traefik reverse proxy setups

## References
- MDN: sameSite=''lax'' is the recommended default
- OWASP: ''lax'' provides sufficient CSRF protection
- Chrome, Firefox: ''lax'' is browser default

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")
